title: Stochastic Path Sampler For Lattice Field Theory
authors: Shiyang Chen; Moxian Qian; Gert Aarts; Biagio Lucini; Kai Zhou
problem: Sample an unnormalized lattice target near criticality without target training data while ordinary Markov chains have long autocorrelations.
method: Learn forward and auxiliary backward Langevin paths by a path-space variational free-energy or entropy-production objective, then correct independent trajectory proposals with extended-space IMH.
method_anchor: PDF pp. 1 and 3-8, abstract and Sec. 2.
result: For the reported 2D phi4 tests, corrected SPS agrees with HMC observables and reports a shorter autocorrelation in the critical region.
scope_boundary: The autocorrelation units differ between IMH steps and HMC trajectories, so the paper does not establish a cost-matched total-efficiency comparison.
safe_sentence: In the reported 2D phi4 benchmark, SPS plus IMH reproduces the reported HMC observables; a cost-matched comparison remains open.
prohibited_sentence: SPS is universally faster than HMC.
next_search: Observable-level errors, autocorrelation, and GPU-hour comparison.

title: Flow-based generative models for Markov chain Monte Carlo in lattice field theory
authors: M. S. Albergo; G. Kanwar; P. E. Shanahan
problem: Reduce critical slowing in lattice-field Markov-chain sampling.
problem_anchor: PDF p. 1 abstract and Sec. I.
method: Train a normalizing-flow proposal and use an independence Metropolis-Hastings chain to correct it to the target distribution.
result: Training improves the reported autocorrelation behavior and the paper compares with HMC and local Metropolis for 2D phi4.
result_anchor: PDF p. 1 abstract and Sec. III.
scope_boundary: The concrete numerical demonstration is 2D phi4; higher-dimensional and QCD scaling are explicitly future-development questions.
scope_anchor: PDF p. 1, end of Sec. I.
safe_sentence: Flow proposals can be coupled to an independence MH correction to retain an exact target chain under the paper's stated conditions.
prohibited_sentence: A learned flow removes critical slowing down in all lattice field theories.
next_search: Compare correction cost and proposal quality across volumes.

title: Equivariant flow-based sampling for lattice gauge theory
authors: Gurtej Kanwar; Michael S. Albergo; Denis Boyda; Kyle Cranmer; Daniel C. Hackett; Sébastien Racanière; Danilo Jimenez Rezende; Phiala E. Shanahan
problem: Construct learned samplers that respect lattice-gauge symmetry and improve topological sampling near criticality.
method: Use gauge-invariant-by-construction, invertible flow-based sampling algorithms.
result: In 2D U(1), the reported approach is orders of magnitude more efficient for topological quantities near critical points than HMC and Heat Bath.
scope_boundary: The stated demonstration is 2D U(1), so it does not by itself prove performance for general gauge groups or QCD.
safe_sentence: Gauge equivariance can be built into flow proposals rather than learned only from data.
prohibited_sentence: This result proves the same efficiency gain for all gauge theories.
next_search: Check modern non-Abelian and volume-scaling results.

title: Path Integral Sampler: a stochastic control approach for sampling
problem: Sample unnormalized densities through a controlled stochastic path rather than an explicit terminal density model.
method: Cast a Schrodinger bridge as stochastic optimal control and use Girsanov-based importance weights to compensate suboptimal control and time discretization.
method_anchor: PDF p. 1 abstract and p. 2 Sec. 1.
result: The paper gives a Wasserstein-distance justification for suboptimal control and reports comparative experiments on several sampling tasks.
scope_boundary: This is a general-sampling paper rather than a lattice-field benchmark, so its relation to SPS is methodological rather than empirical equivalence.
scope_anchor: PDF p. 1 abstract and p. 2 Sec. 1.
safe_sentence: PIS is a path-space control neighbor that preserves an importance-weight correction route for an unnormalized target.
prohibited_sentence: PIS and SPS are the same algorithm.
next_search: Compare their objectives and correction mechanisms on a common lattice benchmark.

title: Stochastic normalizing flows as non-equilibrium transformations
authors: Michele Caselle; Elia Cellini; Alessandro Nada; Marco Panero
problem: Connect generative sampling with nonequilibrium free-energy estimation in lattice field theory.
method: Combine neural normalizing-flow layers with Monte Carlo updates and identify the framework with Jarzynski-based nonequilibrium transformations.
result: The article lays out an efficiency-optimization strategy and applications for this extended generative-model class.
scope_boundary: It establishes a conceptual and algorithmic bridge, not a direct SPS-versus-baseline performance comparison.
safe_sentence: Stochastic normalizing flows provide a documented Jarzynski and nonequilibrium bridge in the SPS method family.
prohibited_sentence: The paper proves that every nonequilibrium learned sampler has the same performance.
next_search: Trace exactness, weights, and numerical costs for each member of the family.

title: Stochastic normalizing flows for lattice field theory
authors: Michele Caselle; Elia Cellini; Alessandro Nada; Marco Panero
problem: Describe hybrid stochastic normalizing flows for sampling Boltzmann distributions in lattice field theory.
method: Combine normalizing flows with Monte Carlo updates in a framework related to Jarzynski equality and nonequilibrium statistical mechanics.
result: The proceeding presents construction details and 2D phi4 applications.
result_anchor: PDF p. 1 abstract and p. 2 Sec. 2.
scope_boundary: This is a short proceeding and should be used as an implementation account, with detailed claims checked against the underlying article.
safe_sentence: The SNF literature explicitly treats deterministic flow layers and stochastic updates as one hybrid sampler family.
prohibited_sentence: This short proceeding establishes a general compute advantage.
next_search: Read the longer JHEP paper for detailed quantitative claims.

title: Denoising Diffusion Samplers
authors: Francisco Vargas; Will Grathwohl; Arnaud Doucet
problem: Sample approximately from unnormalized targets and estimate their normalizing constants through diffusion-style paths.
method: Approximate the reverse of a target-to-Gaussian diffusion and optimize an extended KL or ELBO using an auxiliary stationary reference process.
result: The paper gives theoretical guarantees discussed for DDS and compares sampling methods on challenging non-lattice tasks.
result_anchor: PDF p. 1 abstract and p. 2 Sec. 1.
scope_boundary: DDS is an adjacent unnormalized-target method, not a lattice-field result in this paper.
safe_sentence: DDS is a relevant diffusion and path-measure comparison point for unnormalized targets, not evidence of lattice performance.
prohibited_sentence: DDS validates SPS on lattice phi4.
next_search: Compare target correction and objective form before transferring a claim to lattice systems.

title: Diffusion Models as Stochastic Quantization in Lattice Field Theory
authors: Lingxiao Wang; Gert Aarts; Kai Zhou
problem: Relate generative diffusion models to stochastic quantization and test them as global lattice samplers.
method: Approximate reverse Langevin stochastic dynamics to move from a prior toward the lattice target distribution.
result: The reported 2D phi4 diffusion sampler reduces autocorrelation in the critical region.
scope_boundary: The claim is tied to the tested 2D phi4 setup and the reported model configuration.
safe_sentence: This paper is a lattice diffusion and stochastic-quantization predecessor relevant to SPS's Langevin-path framing.
prohibited_sentence: Diffusion sampling is automatically exact without a correction analysis.
next_search: Compare the sampler's exactness treatment and independent-proposal structure.

title: Out-of-equilibrium simulations to fight topological freezing
authors: Claudio Bonanno; Alessandro Nada; Davide Vadacchino
problem: Mitigate long topological autocorrelations in lattice gauge simulations.
problem_anchor: PDF p. 1 abstract and p. 2 Sec. 1.
method: Use out-of-equilibrium evolutions with open-boundary starting configurations, gradually restore periodic boundaries, and apply a Jarzynski-inspired reweighting-like correction.
result: The contribution reports tests in 2D CP(N-1) and a comparison with parallel tempering on boundary conditions.
result_anchor: PDF p. 1 abstract and p. 2 Sec. 1.
scope_boundary: It is a conference contribution on CP(N-1), and its link to SPS is a shared nonequilibrium family rather than a verified direct root citation.
relation_anchor: Current PDF p. 1; no direct root-reference assertion made.
safe_sentence: Out-of-equilibrium boundary protocols are a nearby route for addressing topological freezing with a correction-like estimator.
prohibited_sentence: This is a direct SPS baseline on the same model.
next_search: Check the later full paper and matched-cost comparisons.

title: Mitigating topological freezing using out-of-equilibrium simulations
authors: Claudio Bonanno; Alessandro Nada; Davide Vadacchino
problem: Mitigate topological freezing using out-of-equilibrium lattice Monte Carlo simulations.
method: Use a Jarzynski-motivated out-of-equilibrium protocol and assess its ESS, KL, topological susceptibility, autocorrelation, and efficiency.
result: For 2D CP(N-1), the reported performance is comparable to the cited parallel-tempering-on-boundary-conditions baseline.
scope_boundary: The article presents a non-ML protocol and says machine-learning combinations are future work; it is not evidence that an SNF or SPS has been tested there.
relation_anchor: Current PDF p. 1; no direct root-reference assertion made.
safe_sentence: This work supplies a concrete nonequilibrium evaluation template beyond ESS alone.
prohibited_sentence: SPS has already been shown to solve topological freezing by this paper.
next_search: Use its observable and cost diagnostics as a comparison template.

title: Variational Autoregressive Networks Applied to $φ^4$ Field Theory Systems
problem: Train and sample discrete Ising and continuous phi4 models without reference data.
method: Combine reinforcement learning with variational autoregressive networks and add single-site or block Metropolis-Hastings corrections to VAN proposals.
method_anchor: PDF p. 1 abstract and contents Sec. 2.4.
result: For the explored ranges, MH corrections reduce residual bias, results agree with standard Monte Carlo within errors, and no clear critical slowing down is observed.
scope_boundary: The evidence is for the reported Ising and phi4 settings and uses ESS alongside corrections; it does not itself establish a universal wall-time win.
safe_sentence: Data-free autoregressive proposals plus MH correction are a direct SPS-family comparison with an explicit residual-bias boundary.
prohibited_sentence: VAN eliminates critical slowing down in all regimes.
next_search: Compare corrected observable errors and training plus sampling cost.

title: Scalable Generative Sampling and Multilevel Estimation for Lattice Field Theories Near Criticality
authors: A. Singha; J. Kauffmann; E. Cellini; K. Jansen; S. Nakajima
problem: Address critical slowing down and scaling limits of single-scale learned lattice samplers near criticality.
problem_anchor: PDF p. 1 abstract and Sec. I.
method: Use a coarse-to-fine multiscale generative sampler with conditional Gaussian mixtures, masked continuous normalizing flows, and exact restriction maps for MLMC.
result: For reported critical 2D phi4 studies, the method has much shorter integrated autocorrelation than HMC, high importance-sampling efficiency, and observables agreeing with long HMC simulations.
scope_boundary: The reported numerical claim is a 2D critical-phi4 result and must not be recast as a general cost-normalized theorem.
relation_anchor: Current PDF p. 1; root predates no citation assertion.
safe_sentence: Multiscale architecture and MLMC are a current alternative route to critical lattice sampling that needs observable-level cost comparison with SPS.
prohibited_sentence: This proves every learned sampler scales favorably at criticality.
next_search: Compare total training, generation, and estimator cost at fixed volume.

title: Operator Spectroscopy of Trained Lattice Samplers
problem: Expose what trained lattice samplers represent internally rather than judging them only by output ensembles.
problem_anchor: PDF p. 1 summary and Sec. I.
method: Project a trained velocity, score, or learned-action residual onto symmetry-fixed operator dictionaries and retain sectors only when held-out and control tests support them.
result: The paper reports separable zero-mode and finite-momentum residual sectors in 2D phi4 and analogous diagnostics for diffusion, normalizing-flow, and gauge-equivariant teachers.
scope_boundary: Operator spectroscopy is a diagnostic of learned functions; it is not by itself a sampling-efficiency benchmark.
scope_anchor: PDF p. 1 summary and Sec. I.
safe_sentence: Operator spectroscopy gives a mechanistic audit that can complement ensemble-level SPS diagnostics.
prohibited_sentence: A low projection residual proves a sampler is exact or fast.
next_search: Apply the diagnostic to a trained SPS model and compare it with observable tests.

title: Diffusion Models for Sampling Near Criticality in Lattice Field Theories
authors: Yang-yang Tan; Gert Aarts; Diaa E. Habibi; Biagio Lucini; Lingxiao Wang
problem: Assess diffusion sampling for 2D and 3D phi4 across phases and test cross-volume transfer near criticality.
problem_anchor: PDF p. 1 abstract and p. 2 Sec. I.
method: Train score-based reverse-SDE samplers and evaluate local score checks, MALA acceptance, HMC-referenced ESS, observable bias and variance, and cross-volume transfer.
result: The reported model reproduces many scalar observables and propagators; residual bias remains in the zero mode and in 3D action density, with one reported broken-phase susceptibility exception.
scope_boundary: This is a July 2026 forward neighbor of SPS, and its explicit residuals show why a high summary metric alone is insufficient.
relation_anchor: Published after the SPS root; no backward citation is asserted.
safe_sentence: This paper provides a current evaluation template combining observables, local diagnostics, and transfer checks.
prohibited_sentence: The diffusion model is unbiased for every observable and volume.
next_search: Use its diagnostic bundle when designing SPS comparisons.
